shelf_mark: Paris, Bibliothèque nationale de France, Département des Manuscrits, Grec 2485
note_on_numbering: Earlier scholarship sometimes cited as 'Paris. gr. 2348' — the correct BnF shelfmark is Grec 2485 (Tannery's siglum A).
contents: Diophantus of Alexandria, Arithmetica Books I–VI
Diophantus of Alexandria, On Polygonal Numbers
place_of_copy: Italy (Rome or Ferrara, dated by watermark Briquet no. 6291)
layout: Two columns for Diophantus text; scholia by Planudes in full-page width
scribal_marks: Rubricated: book titles + initial E of each section; sigil σχ marks scholia openings; custodes at end of each gathering
blank_spaces: Several blank spaces preserving lost figures not transmitted from antigraph
provenance: Stamp: XVIIe siècle, type Josserand-Bruno n. 4 (ff. 1r, 214v)
Binding: XIXe siècle, marbled boards, red maroquin spine with Louis-Philippe cipher, binder: Duplanil

tannery_edition.title: Diophanti Alexandrini Opera Omnia cum graecis commentariis

heath_study.title: Diophantus of Alexandria: A Study in the History of Greek Algebra
heath_study.author: Sir Thomas L. Heath

pinakes.note: Search Pinakes at https://pinakes.irht.cnrs.fr for full MS tradition of Arithmetica

research_significance: Tannery's siglum A — the primary Greek archetype used for all modern critical editions of the Arithmetica. Contains the syncopated algebraic notation that is the earliest known use of algebraic symbolism in European mathematics. Planudes' scholia are the earliest Byzantine mathematical commentary on Diophantus.
